title: 2026 Fatboy Bikes Bagus V3
description:
Matte grey Fatboy Bagus V3 (500W BAFANG hub motor, 52V 20Ah Samsung battery, 7-speed Shimano, Tektro hydraulic disc brakes). Family/two-seater configuration with a rear child seat fitted at time of theft. Serial 355522520014599 stamped on the handlebar stem. Purchased new 29 Dec 2025 from Eozzie Electric Vehicles, Altona North VIC (invoice #3468). Registered with Fatboy Bikes 29 Jan 2026.
STOLEN 15 April 2026 at approx 07:36 AM from 12 Avoca Street, Bondi NSW 2026. Suspect captured on CCTV; also stole a skateboard from 34 Avoca Street earlier the same morning. Possible getaway: white van with 2–3 young men seen at the mouth of Avoca Lane.
Reported: NSW Police Event E87922331, Officer Wilson (55998@police.nsw.gov.au). Also listed on BikeVAULT. DO NOT BUY — contact NSW Police immediately if seen. Owner: anica.lario@gmail.com / 0433 792 117.

title: 2010 Trek Gary Fisher
description:
Post for seat pole damaged. 
A man my age was yelling at it with the seat off, and I asled him what was wrong. He said the seat wouldn't tighten. I observed the frame damage and figured I could make it at least RIDEABLE for him. We walk it back to my apartment, and he starts getting all weirded out, as I'm working on it. He says he better leave, and I say that it's literally rideable until he can find a repair shop. He says "It's your bike now." 
Which leads me to believe it's stolen. I called Eugene, OR PD, and the serial numbers I gave them weren't showing up anywhere.

title: 2009 GT Bicycles Transeo
description: I bought my bike in 2013 off Craigslist from a women who was upgrading to a nicer commuting bike. I think it's a 2009 based on the frame, color, and parts. It has EF50 L shimano shifters, which weren't used on the 2011 and 2012 models. I've added cheapo fenders to make riding in Tacoma and Seattle more pleasant. We've gone on tours around many of the parks here. I also rode it in Portland after taking it on the Amtrak- saaaweeeeet! I love my bike and have decorated it with stickers favorite shops around the area. My bike was stolen in 2015 and rediscovered at a pawn shop the next day. I'm so lucky to have this bike and i can imagine losing it again!

title: 2020 REI Co-op DRT 2.1 Mountain Bike
description: The bike is a Co-op Cycles DRT 2.1 mountain bike, size XL, with a 6061 aluminum hardtail frame in the stock REI color scheme. It has a Suntour SR AIR-Boost front suspension fork with 140 mm travel, a SRAM SX Eagle 1×12 drivetrain with an 11–50T cassette, and Tektro Auriga hydraulic disc brakes front and rear. The wheels are 27.5-inch with 2.8-inch wide tires, tubeless-ready rims, upgraded pedals to black Chester pedals . It is equipped with an X-Fusion Manic dropper seatpost with a handlebar-mounted remote. The bike also features a WTB Volt Sport saddle, riser handlebars, and weighs about 34 pounds in this XL frame size.

title: 2021 Carbon Master-handbike Handbike-recumbent
description: This is actually a customized handbike for a full time manual wheelchair athlete. The full carbon frame has decals that read “Carbon Master” in green and white. The bike has one training (clincher) wheel in the front, size 26’ (may still have a label on the wheel that says “Kara”). It has two small 20’ rear wheels. The bike has an SRM power adapter in the cranks and SRAM electronic shifting. Braking is v-brake style, one that is attached to the hand cranks and an emergency break underneath the fork. The handbike also has a thin black pad in the bucket (the part you lay on). The back of the bucket where your head would rest has a large hole in the frame to carry water etc.

title: 2013 Surly Moonlander
description:
Upgraded 2013 Surly Moonlander (Large, 20" seat tube). Dark (Space?) black/gray metallic paint (white decals), some rust at headset, mounts and bottom bracket. Offset fork (slight "S' bend) due to narrow wheel hubs. Front hub has additional threading on one side. Original Clown Shoe rims w black rim liner. Bontrager Barbegazi (?) tires 27.5 x 4.5" (prob have red iron dust on them). Upgraded gruppo: single front ring (orig was double ring), massive rear 11 or 12 speed cassette with "stump puller" type cog. Sram Level T hydraulic disc brakes (o.g. were cable actuated Shimano). Ergo grips on flat mountain style handlebar. 45North pedals, black with orange spikes/teeth (some worn and broken off). orig WTB saddle.

Posted photos are prior to upgrades.

Surly Moonlander, Surly 4130 CroMoly steel. Main triangle double-butted. TIG-welded

title: 2020 Specialized Diverge
description: Orange bar tape, Terra carbon wheels 38mm Pathfinder pros. Topeak micro rocket pump, crank bothers pedals, sram apex, literally a completely worn out drive train, selle Italia super boost saddle, red reflector on seat post, fox sticker on headtube, handup stickers matching on each side of seat post tube, faded party time sticker inside of rear stansions, downtube protector peeling off bottom, hand written note inside seat post "this is my bike bitch - Tyler Oshnock" damage through out, 20k miles on the frame every piece of it is scratched and chipped paint throughout, distinct wear "strips" from touring frame bags, seat post clamp is reversed. The whole bike is basically used up besides the frame tires and wheels, It's been over 20,000 miles all over the united states. I miss it dearly already.
